concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes (vertices) connected by edges.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A link between two vertices in a graph.

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A point or element in a graph.

concepts.name: Weighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where each edge has an associated numerical value (weight).

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have no direction.

concepts.name: Connected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where there is a path between any two vertices.

concepts.name: Minimum Spanning Tree
concepts.short_definition: A subset of edges of a connected, edge-weighted undirected graph that connects all the vertices together, without any cycles and with the minimum possible total edge weight.

concepts.name: Spanning Tree
concepts.short_definition: A tree that includes all the vertices of a graph.

concepts.name: Tree
concepts.short_definition: A connected graph with no cycles.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A path in a graph that starts and ends at the same vertex and visits other vertices in between.

concepts.name: Path
concepts.short_definition: A sequence of vertices connected by edges.

concepts.name: Kruskal's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A greedy algorithm that finds a minimum spanning tree for a connected, edge-weighted undirected graph.

concepts.name: Greedy Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that makes the locally optimal choice at each stage with the hope of finding a global optimum.

concepts.name: Edge Sorting
concepts.short_definition: Arranging the edges of a graph in ascending order of their weights.

concepts.name: Disjoint Set Union
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that keeps track of a set of elements partitioned into a number of disjoint (non-overlapping) subsets.

concepts.name: Union Operation
concepts.short_definition: Merges two subsets into a single subset in a Disjoint Set Union data structure.

concepts.name: Find Operation
concepts.short_definition: Determines which subset a particular element belongs to in a Disjoint Set Union data structure.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: The process of identifying if a graph contains any cycles.

concepts.name: Adding Edges
concepts.short_definition: The process of selecting and adding edges to form the MST.

concepts.name: Iterating Through Edges
concepts.short_definition: Processing each edge of the graph in a specific order.

concepts.name: Weight Comparison
concepts.short_definition: Comparing the numerical values associated with edges.

concepts.name: Subset
concepts.short_definition: A collection of elements that is part of a larger set.

concepts.name: Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection of distinct objects.

concepts.name: Connectivity Check
concepts.short_definition: Verifying if adding an edge would create a cycle or connect previously disconnected components.

concepts.name: MST Construction
concepts.short_definition: The step-by-step process of assembling the Minimum Spanning Tree.

concepts.name: Algorithm Correctness
concepts.short_definition: Proof or reasoning that an algorithm produces the correct output.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of time an algorithm takes to run as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of memory an algorithm uses as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Prim's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: Another greedy algorithm for finding a minimum spanning tree.

concepts.name: Comparison with Prim's
concepts.short_definition: Analyzing the differences and similarities between Kruskal's and Prim's algorithms.

concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Ways to store graph data, such as adjacency list or adjacency matrix.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where each vertex has a list of its adjacent vertices.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where a matrix indicates the presence of edges between vertices.

concepts.name: Edge List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph as a list of all its edges.

concepts.name: Forest
concepts.short_definition: A collection of disjoint trees.

concepts.name: Component
concepts.short_definition: A subgraph in which any two vertices are connected to each other by paths, and which is connected to no additional vertices in the supergraph.

concepts.name: Path Compression
concepts.short_definition: An optimization technique for the Find operation in Disjoint Set Union.

concepts.name: Union by Rank
concepts.short_definition: An optimization technique for the Union operation in Disjoint Set Union.

concepts.name: Applications of MST
concepts.short_definition: Real-world problems that can be solved using Minimum Spanning Trees.
